title: Language Learning Cost Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Monthly cost to learn a language
description: Compare language learning costs by method. Find your budget. Free calculator with 2026 pricing.
intro: Learning a language requires hundreds of hours of deliberate exposure. According to the FSI (Foreign Service Institute), for English speakers: Spanish, French, or Italian ~600–900 hours; German ~1100 hours; Arabic, Mandarin Chinese ~2200+ hours. Strategy: consistency beats intensity. Budget varies by method—apps, online tutoring, academy, or full immersion. Our calculator is fast and free. All data is current for 2026 and updated regularly.
keyTakeaway: **Monthly budget by learning method.** Apps: $0–20/month; Online tutoring: $100–300/month; Language academy: $80–200/month; Full immersion abroad: $1500–3000/month. Choose based on your timeline and resources.

explanation:
**Learning a language** is a sustained effort spanning hundreds of hours. Cognitive science research—including Ebbinghaus spacing and spaced repetition—combined with immersion and active practice are the proven pillars supported by FSI and Cambridge.

## How it's calculated

Language learning costs range from free (community exchange) to $3000+/month (premium full immersion programs). This calculator estimates your monthly expenses based on your chosen method, with validation for realistic cost ranges.

## Important notes

This is an estimate for planning purposes. All pricing reflects 2026 market rates and is reviewed regularly. For critical financial decisions, consult a professional.

faq.q: How much does it cost to learn a language?
faq.a: It depends on your method. Apps like Duolingo cost $0–20/month, online tutoring $100–300/month, academies $80–200/month, and full immersion programs $1500–3000+/month.

faq.q: Is Duolingo the cheapest way to learn a language?
faq.a: It's one of the most affordable at $0–20/month, but works best combined with other methods. Free language exchange and community meetups are also available.

faq.q: How much should I budget for language learning?
faq.a: Budget $20–100/month for casual learning, $100–300/month for structured progress, or $1500+/month for immersion or intensive programs.

faq.q: What's the best value for language learning?
faq.a: A hybrid approach: combine an affordable app ($10–15/month) with 1–2 weekly tutoring sessions ($50–100/month) for the best balance of cost and results.

faq.q: How long does it take to reach fluency?
faq.a: FSI estimates: Spanish ~600–750 hours, French ~900 hours, Mandarin ~2200+ hours. With consistent practice, expect 1–3 years depending on intensity and method.
